# GEO Repair

> GEO Repair runs a free checkup on your website to score how ready it is for AI search engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews, then opens a pull request that fixes what's holding you back. No code required.

GEO Repair (publicly: "AI Search Optimization") scores a site across 23 checks in 7 categories: rendering, structured data, metadata, crawl surface, semantics, content, and answerability. An AI agent then clones the one GitHub repository you pick into an ephemeral sandbox, applies the fixes, and opens a pull request for your review. Your code is never retained and is never used to train models.

Every page on this site has a clean Markdown twin: append `.md` to its URL (the home page is at /index.md) for a faithful, machine-readable copy. The `.md` links below point to those twins.

## Core pages

- [Home](https://geo.repair/) ([.md](https://geo.repair/index.md)): Free AI search readiness checkup and automatic fix PRs.
- [GEO & AEO checker](https://geo.repair/geo-aeo-checker) ([.md](https://geo.repair/geo-aeo-checker.md)): Free online GEO and AEO checker: test AI citation and AI visibility readiness.
- [Security](https://geo.repair/security) ([.md](https://geo.repair/security.md)): Zero data retention, no model training, ephemeral sandbox, least-privilege single-repo scope.
- [Pricing](https://geo.repair/pricing) ([.md](https://geo.repair/pricing.md)): Free checkup, a one-time fix sized to your sitemap (from $49), and optional $19/mo Autopilot.
- [Contact](https://geo.repair/contact) ([.md](https://geo.repair/contact.md)): Get in touch with the GEO Repair team.

## Resources

- [Blog](https://geo.repair/blog) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog.md)): Guides on AI search optimization and how AI crawlers read your site.
- [Terms](https://geo.repair/terms) ([.md](https://geo.repair/terms.md)): Terms of service.
- [Privacy](https://geo.repair/privacy) ([.md](https://geo.repair/privacy.md)): Privacy policy: zero retention, no training on your code.

## Blog posts

- [Is There a Tool That Optimizes AEO, SEO, and GEO for My Website?](https://geo.repair/blog/tool-optimize-aeo-seo-geo-website) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/tool-optimize-aeo-seo-geo-website.md)): Yes, but the useful tool changes the website, not just the score. Here is what an AEO, SEO, and GEO tool should diagnose, fix, and verify.
- [Is There an AI Tool That Actually Fixes AEO and GEO Issues?](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-tool-actually-fixes-aeo-geo-issues) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-tool-actually-fixes-aeo-geo-issues.md)): Most tools report AI visibility problems. A real fixing tool works at the website source level, opens a pull request, and re-checks the result.
- [AEO and GEO Audit Tools vs Fix Tools: What Is the Difference?](https://geo.repair/blog/aeo-geo-audit-tool-vs-fix-tool) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/aeo-geo-audit-tool-vs-fix-tool.md)): An audit tool tells you what is wrong. A fix tool changes the website and verifies the blocker was removed. Here is when to use each one.
- [AI Search Optimization Checklist for Technical Teams](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-search-optimization-checklist) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-search-optimization-checklist.md)): A practical checklist for making a site easier for ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews to fetch, parse, and trust.
- [How to Increase Your Chances of Being Cited in AI Answers](https://geo.repair/blog/increase-ai-citation-chances) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/increase-ai-citation-chances.md)): A realistic answer to a common Reddit question: what content structure, crawlability, and source signals can make a page easier for AI systems to cite.
- [Is Traditional SEO Enough for AI Overviews?](https://geo.repair/blog/traditional-seo-ai-overviews) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/traditional-seo-ai-overviews.md)): Traditional SEO still matters, but AI answers reward clearer extraction, entity trust, and sourceable content. Here is where the work changes.
- [AI Mentions vs AI Citations: What Should You Track?](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-mentions-vs-citations) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-mentions-vs-citations.md)): A brand mention and a cited URL are not the same signal. Track them separately so you know whether you have awareness, retrieval, or both.
- [Should Solo Founders Automate Blog Publishing with AI?](https://geo.repair/blog/automated-ai-blog-publishing) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/automated-ai-blog-publishing.md)): AI can speed up planning and drafts, but automatic publishing without review creates thin content, duplicate intent, and trust problems.
- [Why Traffic Drops Even When Rankings Look Good](https://geo.repair/blog/rankings-good-traffic-dropping-ai-search) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/rankings-good-traffic-dropping-ai-search.md)): Stable rankings can hide falling clicks when AI answers satisfy the query on the results page. Diagnose impressions, CTR, and answer visibility together.
- [Is AI Search Optimization Just SEO?](https://geo.repair/blog/is-ai-search-optimization-just-seo) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/is-ai-search-optimization-just-seo.md)): AI Search Optimization is not a replacement for SEO. It is an extra readiness layer focused on extraction, answerability, structured data, and trust.
- [What Google Search Console Cannot Tell You About AI Search](https://geo.repair/blog/google-search-console-ai-search-gaps) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/google-search-console-ai-search-gaps.md)): Search Console is still useful, but it does not fully explain ChatGPT, Perplexity, or cross-platform AI recommendations. Here is what to measure beside it.
- [Do AI Overviews Kill Organic Traffic?](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-overviews-organic-traffic) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-overviews-organic-traffic.md)): AI Overviews can reduce clicks on some informational queries, but the useful question is which pages lose demand and which pages become citation sources.
- [How to Get Recommended by ChatGPT and Perplexity](https://geo.repair/blog/chatgpt-perplexity-business-recommendations) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/chatgpt-perplexity-business-recommendations.md)): AI recommendations depend on clear entity signals, third-party proof, and category sources. Your homepage is only one part of the recommendation system.
- [What Should an AI Search Optimization Tool Actually Do?](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-search-optimization-tool-checklist) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-search-optimization-tool-checklist.md)): The useful tool is not another content generator. It should diagnose why a page is invisible, recommend the fix, and verify whether the fix worked.
- [Is SEO Still Worth It in 2026?](https://geo.repair/blog/is-seo-worth-it-2026) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/is-seo-worth-it-2026.md)): SEO is still worth doing, but the work has shifted from keyword shortcuts toward trust, community proof, useful content, and AI-readable structure.
- [The SEO Mistakes Teams Make After Switching to AI Content](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-content-seo-mistakes) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-content-seo-mistakes.md)): Publishing faster is not a strategy. The common failures are generic drafts, cannibalization, weak review, missing experience, and no sourceable point of view.
- [How to Show Experience and Trust in AI Search Content](https://geo.repair/blog/eeat-ai-search-trust) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/eeat-ai-search-trust.md)): Trust is easier to claim than prove. Show real experience through examples, author context, original data, dated updates, and off-site reputation signals.
- [What Content Is Hardest for AI Answers to Replace?](https://geo.repair/blog/content-ai-answers-cannot-replace) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/content-ai-answers-cannot-replace.md)): No content is fully AI-proof, but original data, tools, templates, case studies, and community proof are harder to replace than generic explainers.
- [AI Translation Is Not International SEO](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-translation-international-seo) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-translation-international-seo.md)): AI translation can make localization faster, but international SEO still needs local intent research, hreflang accuracy, market examples, and human review.
- [How to Write Titles and Meta Descriptions for Google and AI Answers](https://geo.repair/blog/titles-meta-descriptions-ai-answers) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/titles-meta-descriptions-ai-answers.md)): The best titles and descriptions are specific, human, and aligned with the first paragraph. Clarity beats keyword stuffing for both search and AI answers.
- [How to Build Topical Authority for AI Search](https://geo.repair/blog/topical-authority-ai-search) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/topical-authority-ai-search.md)): Topical authority is less about publishing every keyword variation and more about connected, useful, experience-backed pages that prove real expertise.
- [Why Server-Rendered Content Matters for AI Search](https://geo.repair/blog/server-rendered-content-ai-search) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/server-rendered-content-ai-search.md)): AI search engines can only quote what they can fetch. Learn why server-rendered HTML is the foundation of AI search readiness.
- [Structured Data for AI Search: What to Add First](https://geo.repair/blog/structured-data-for-ai-search) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/structured-data-for-ai-search.md)): A focused guide to the JSON-LD that helps AI search engines understand articles, organizations, breadcrumbs, products, and FAQs.
- [Robots.txt for AI Crawlers: What to Allow and What to Avoid](https://geo.repair/blog/robots-txt-ai-crawlers) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/robots-txt-ai-crawlers.md)): How to keep important pages open to AI crawlers without accidentally exposing private, staging, or low-value surfaces.
- [What Is llms.txt and Should Your Website Have One?](https://geo.repair/blog/llms-txt-for-ai-search) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/llms-txt-for-ai-search.md)): A practical explanation of llms.txt, Markdown twins, and how a curated machine-readable index can support AI search readiness.
- [How to Write Answer-First Content for AI Search](https://geo.repair/blog/answer-first-content-ai-search) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/answer-first-content-ai-search.md)): Question-shaped headings, short direct answers, and evidence-rich sections help AI systems understand what a page is qualified to answer.
- [AI Search Optimization for SaaS Websites](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-search-optimization-for-saas) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/ai-search-optimization-for-saas.md)): The SaaS pages most likely to be read by AI search engines, and the technical fixes that make pricing, security, and feature pages clearer.
- [How to Audit a Next.js Site for AI Search Readiness](https://geo.repair/blog/next-js-ai-search-audit) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/next-js-ai-search-audit.md)): A route-by-route audit process for checking rendered HTML, metadata, structured data, crawl files, and content clarity in a Next.js app.
- [How AI Crawlers Read Your Site](https://geo.repair/blog/how-ai-crawlers-read-your-site) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/how-ai-crawlers-read-your-site.md)): ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews fetch your pages differently than a browser. Here's what their crawlers see, and why server-rendered HTML wins.
- [Product Page AI Search Readiness Checklist](https://geo.repair/blog/product-pages-ai-search-readiness) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/product-pages-ai-search-readiness.md)): How to make product and feature pages easier for AI search engines to understand without stuffing keywords or promising citations.
- [How to Measure AI Search Readiness Before and After a Fix](https://geo.repair/blog/measure-ai-search-readiness) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/measure-ai-search-readiness.md)): A practical measurement framework for tracking crawl visibility, metadata quality, structured data, answerability, and technical readiness.
- [What Is AI Search Optimization?](https://geo.repair/blog/what-is-ai-search-optimization) ([.md](https://geo.repair/blog/what-is-ai-search-optimization.md)): A plain-English definition of AI Search Optimization: how it differs from classic SEO, and the checks that actually move the needle.
